name: Exchange Bank & Trust
description_short: FDIC-insured bank offering personal, business, and military checking/savings accounts, loans, mortgages, and credit cards with locations in Kansas.
description_long:
Exchange Bank & Trust is a traditional community bank headquartered in Kansas with a demonstrated history of over 160 years serving local farmers and residents. The bank holds FDIC insurance backing, providing deposit protection up to regulatory limits. They maintain physical locations in Atchison, Leavenworth, and Basehor, Kansas, and offer comprehensive financial services across personal, business, and military customer segments.

The bank provides a full suite of deposit products including multiple checking account tiers (Flex, Rewards, 50 Plus Interest, Commercial, Military variants), savings accounts, money market accounts, and CDs. Loan offerings span personal term loans, auto loans, home mortgages, HELOCs, agricultural loans, commercial real estate financing, equipment loans, business lines of credit, working capital loans, and SBA loans. They also issue personal and business credit cards, provide investment services, and offer Medicare solutions for qualifying customers.

Exchange Bank & Trust differentiates itself through specialized account structures for distinct demographics: senior-focused 50 Plus accounts, youth savings accounts, military-exclusive checking with preferential rates, and agricultural lending expertise spanning 160+ years. They provide digital banking capabilities, person-to-person payments, remote deposit capture, merchant services, and cash management tools. The bank emphasizes accessibility (AudioEye enabled website) and community involvement through their published Community Reinvestment Act file.

As a regional community bank, Exchange Bank & Trust is best suited for customers seeking relationship banking with local branch access in the Kansas tri-city area. The main caveat is geographic limitation—their physical footprint appears concentrated in northeastern Kansas, which may not serve customers in other states or those preferring nationwide branch networks. Online banking and digital-first consumers may find larger national banks more feature-rich. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts (Flex, Rewards, 50 Plus, Interest)
Business checking and commercial checking accounts
Military checking accounts (Advantage, Executive, Interest, Rewards variants)
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) for personal and business
Home mortgage loans and home equity lines of credit (HELOC)
SBA loans and small business financing
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full faith and credit of U.S. Government backing
Specialized military checking accounts with dedicated rates and services for active duty and veterans
Over 160 years of agricultural lending expertise and community farming relationships
Multiple account tiers designed for specific demographics (50+ seniors, youth, military, small business)
Comprehensive loan products including SBA loans, equipment financing, and commercial real estate
Free pre-approval on auto loans to help customers negotiate from strength
Digital banking, person-to-person payments, and remote deposit capture capabilities
Accessibility-focused website with AudioEye toolbar and optimization features
cons: Geographic limitation: Physical locations only in Atchison, Leavenworth, and Basehor, Kansas—not suitable for out-of-state customers needing branch access
Limited information on website regarding specific APR/rates, fees, or loan terms to compare competitively
No mention of mobile app features, mobile check deposit specifics, or modern digital banking parity with national banks
Credit card section shows 'Sorry, content not available'—offerings not detailed on website
No information provided about minimum account balances, overdraft fees, or monthly maintenance charges
best_for: Kansas-based small business owners seeking relationship lending and agricultural expertise
Active military members and veterans in northeastern Kansas seeking specialized military accounts
Farmers and agricultural businesses leveraging 160+ years of farm lending experience
Customers ages 50+ in the bank's service area seeking senior-focused checking and savings products
diagnosis: Exchange Bank & Trust is best for Kansas-area customers (particularly farmers, seniors, military, and small business owners) seeking relationship banking with FDIC insurance and community expertise. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—their service footprint appears confined to northeastern Kansas, making them unsuitable for customers outside this region or those requiring nationwide branch access.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
